title: Transport Segregation via Shared Service Core — `/_site` BFF + `/samantha/v2` public, with one Service per domain
scope:
Adopt a shared service core with two thin transport adapters as the single architectural pattern for every Vector domain. One Go Service per domain holds all logic and DB access; two HTTP mounts sit on top — /_site for site/staff BFF traffic, /samantha/v2 for the customer-facing public API. Both mounts call the same Service methods. This is the win-win: site features keep shipping at full speed (no detour, no dual-stack) while customer surface stays cleanly segregated by URL prefix, lint, and DTO boundary.

Concretely: (1) mount /_site in main.go and re-home every site-only route under it; (2) rename frontend helper apiInfra → apiSite pointing at the new prefix; (3) introduce two lints — lint:public-helper-allowlist (caller-side gate on api()/apiV2) and lint:no-db-in-handlers (writer-side ratchet against pool imports in handlers); (4) extract the eight handler stragglers (auth, fields, errorsreport, libraryreleases, roles, portfoliomodels ×3, portfolio/master_record) onto their services so the lint can flip from warn-list to allow-list-empty; (5) ship the audit_events table + audit.Service.Record() sole-writer with source_transport ∈ {site, public} for SOC 2 evidence; (6) introduce a dto.go + MapPublic mapper convention guarded by lint:public-dto-mapper to stop internal columns leaking through the customer adapter; (7) document everything in docs/c_c_transport_segregation.md.

Out of scope: rewriting any service; GraphQL; multi-region; tenant-per-DB; gateway selection (the gateway rule lands in B17.9 / a follow-up plan).
value:
Today, the site is overwhelmingly riding the customer pool: the frontend split is 252 api() / 9 apiV2 / 8 apiInfra calls. The Reset Adoption State 404 Rick hit on 2026-05-09 was a direct consequence of that confusion — the BFF and the public API were indistinguishable to the developer. Without an explicit boundary, every new site feature accidentally enlarges the customer attack surface, every new public field risks exposing internal columns, and SOC 2 reviewers will not be able to point at a single audit boundary.

The win-win shape solves all three problems without slowing site delivery. Building a site feature still looks the same — write a handler, call a Service. The only new constraint is which router subtree the handler is mounted under, which is a one-line decision per route. In return: (a) URL prefix becomes a usable security signal (gateway rules, WAF, log filters, on-call dashboards); (b) one audit_events table records both site and customer mutations with source_transport distinguishing them — a SOC 2 reviewer can see the entire boundary on one screen; (c) the DTO mapper convention makes accidental column leakage a lint failure, not a postmortem. The B21 substrate (artefactitemsv2) and the existing 18 service.go files are already the right shape — this plan ratchets them in place rather than rewriting anything.
implementation_plan: Task 1: Mount /_site in backend/cmd/server/main.go — chi Route block re-homing /admin, /me, /nav, /auth/refresh + /auth/logout, /dev, /healthz, /env*, /page-help, /library/releases, /custom-pages, /user/tab-order, /addressables, /errors, /workspaces, /status/pipeline. Keep root-level shims emitting `Deprecation: site=/_site` header for ≤2 release cycles.
Task 2: Frontend helper rename — apiInfra → apiSite in app/lib/api.ts; change base URL to API_INFRA_BASE + '/_site'; codemod the 8 call sites.

Task 3: Bug-fix dev/pages/DevPage.tsx:157 — switch from `api('/api/admin/dev/adoption-reset')` to `apiSite('/admin/dev/adoption-reset')` (drops stale /api prefix and fixes today's 404). Verifies the new pattern end-to-end.

Task 4: Lint lint:public-helper-allowlist — dev/scripts/lint_public_helper_allowlist.py + dev/registries/public_helper_allowlist.txt (seed with current customer-facing files only). Wire into npm run lint:public-helper-allowlist; add to CI gate.

Task 5: Lint lint:no-db-in-handlers — dev/scripts/lint_no_db_in_handlers.py + dev/registries/handler_db_exemptions.txt (seed with auth, fields, errorsreport, libraryreleases, roles, portfoliomodels handlers ×3, portfolio/master_record_handler). Wire into npm + CI.
Task 6: Extract auth/handler.go → auth.Service. Methods: Login, Refresh, Logout. Remove auth from db-exemptions ledger. Remount under /_site/auth.

Task 7: Extract fields/handler.go → fields.Service. Custom-field CRUD methods. Remove from ledger. Decide: site-only or both? (Field library is staff-managed; pin to /_site/fields with public read-only on /samantha/v2/fields.)
Task 8: Extract errorsreport/handler.go → errorsreport.Service. Move under /_site/errors (site-only). Wire audit.Record once Task 11 lands.
Task 9: Extract libraryreleases/handler.go → libraryreleases.Service. Cross-DB pool stays in service. Mount /_site/library/releases.
Task 10: Extract roles/handler.go reads → roles.Service.List/Get/etc. (Service already exists for writes per lint:writer-boundary.) Remove from ledger.
Task 11: Extract portfoliomodels/handler*.go (×3) and portfolio/master_record_handler.go → services. Coordinate with PLA-0026 to avoid file-collision; bundle into one PR.

Task 12: audit_events table + audit.Service.Record() — db/schema/NNN_audit_events.sql with (id, tenant_id, actor_user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, request_id, source_transport, before_jsonb, after_jsonb, created_at). Service in backend/internal/audit/. Extend lint:writer-boundary with audit_events → audit.Service.

Task 13: Wire audit.Record into mutating service methods — start with the three highest-traffic services (artefactitemsv2, roles, orgdesign). Synchronous, in-transaction; failure rolls back the parent.
Task 14: DTO + mapper convention — every service exposing data via /samantha/v2 gets a dto.go with `MapPublic(internal X) dto.XPublic`. Document the rule in docs/c_c_transport_segregation.md.

Task 15: Lint lint:public-dto-mapper — dev/scripts/lint_public_dto_mapper.py: any handler under app/api/v2/* (or backend/internal/*/handler*.go that registers under /samantha/v2) returning a Go struct from internal/<svc> root (not internal/<svc>/dto) fails. Wire into npm + CI.

Task 16: Docs — write docs/c_c_transport_segregation.md (diagram of handler→Service→audit, the URL-prefix rule, the three lints, the DTO convention, the SOC 2 evidence story). Add one-line pointer in CLAUDE.md alongside c_c_v1_v2_cutover.md.
Task 17: Stories skill — extend the <stories> checklist so every new endpoint card declares `transport: site | public | both`. Update .claude/skills/stories/SKILL.md and the gate doc.

Task 18: Tests — Playwright E2E run after each handler extraction (Tasks 6–11): full smoke (login, dashboard, work-items, portfolio, dev page) to catch route-shim regressions before deprecation header expires.
areas_impacted: Backend: backend/cmd/server/main.go — new chi Route('/_site') block + root-level shims for back-compat window
Backend: backend/internal/audit/ (new package) — Service + Record() + lint:writer-boundary entry
Backend: every backend/internal/<svc>/ that returns data via /samantha/v2 — new dto.go + MapPublic mapper
Frontend: app/lib/api.ts — apiInfra → apiSite rename + base URL change
Frontend: 8 existing apiInfra call sites — codemod to apiSite
Frontend: dev/pages/DevPage.tsx:157 — adoption-reset path fix (the smoke test for Task 1)
Lints: dev/scripts/lint_public_helper_allowlist.py, lint_no_db_in_handlers.py, lint_public_dto_mapper.py — three new scripts
Lint ledgers: dev/registries/public_helper_allowlist.txt, handler_db_exemptions.txt — two new ledgers
feature_list: /_site BFF subtree mounted in main.go with all site-only routes re-homed
Frontend helper trio: api() (legacy v1), apiV2() (customer public), apiSite() (BFF) — names match what they do
lint:public-helper-allowlist gating api() and apiV2 callers behind a vetted ledger
lint:no-db-in-handlers blocking pgxpool/database/sql import in non-test handler files
Eight handler stragglers extracted onto their service packages — db-exemptions ledger empties
audit_events table with source_transport ∈ {site, public} for SOC 2 evidence
DTO + MapPublic mapper convention on every service exposing data via /samantha/v2
lint:public-dto-mapper guarding against internal-struct leakage on the public adapter
Per-service dto.go pattern documented and enforced
docs/c_c_transport_segregation.md as the single architectural reference for the split
<stories> skill extended with `transport: site | public | both` decision gate per endpoint card
Deprecation: site=/_site header on root-level shims for ≤2 release cycles before removal
Adoption-Reset 404 fixed (the smoke test that proved why this plan exists)
features_extended: B17.9 (api gateway) — gains a usable signal: prefix /_site can be routed/blocked at gateway level
B8.10 (scoped api keys) — tenant API keys become naturally constrained to /samantha/v2 since /_site is staff-only
B8.7 (idempotency) — applies to /samantha/v2 only; /_site can stay simpler
PLA-0030 (v1→v2 cutover) — every v1 route either retires or moves under /_site or /samantha/v2; no third option
PLA-0007 (RBAC) — staff-permission gates concentrate on /_site; tenant-permission gates on /samantha/v2
PLA-0019 (Samantha External API Surface) — OpenAPI spec covers only /samantha/v2; /_site is intentionally undocumented externally
features_removed: Frontend helper apiInfra (renamed to apiSite as part of Task 2 — name kept short and intent-revealing)
Implicit assumption that 'apiInfra means infra-y stuff' — replaced with explicit 'apiSite means the site BFF, period'
Root-level mounting of site-only routes (after deprecation window expires post-Task 1)
Handler-direct DB pool imports in the eight extracted packages

work_item_backlog.title: Mount `/_site` BFF subtree in main.go and re-home site-only routes
work_item_backlog.description: As a backend engineer, I wish to mount a `/_site` chi Route block in `backend/cmd/server/main.go` that re-homes every site-only route (`/admin`, `/me`, `/nav`, `/auth/refresh`+`/logout`, `/dev`, `/healthz`, `/env*`, `/page-help`, `/library/releases`, `/custom-pages`, `/user/tab-order`, `/addressables`, `/errors`, `/workspaces`, `/status/pipeline`) under it, so that the BFF surface is identifiable by URL prefix at the gateway, in logs, and in WAF rules. Keep root-level shims emitting `Deprecation: site=/_site` header for ≤2 release cycles.

work_item_backlog.title: Rename frontend helper `apiInfra` → `apiSite`; point at `/_site`
work_item_backlog.description: As a frontend engineer, I wish to rename `apiInfra` to `apiSite` in `app/lib/api.ts` and change its base URL to `API_INFRA_BASE + '/_site'`, so that the helper name matches its purpose (BFF) and every existing call site routes through the new mount. Codemod the 8 existing call sites.

work_item_backlog.title: Lint `lint:public-helper-allowlist` — gate `api()` / `apiV2` callers behind a vetted ledger
work_item_backlog.description: As a devex engineer, I wish to add `dev/scripts/lint_public_helper_allowlist.py` and `dev/registries/public_helper_allowlist.txt` so that any new file calling `api()` or `apiV2()` outside the ledger fails CI, forcing deliberate decisions about customer-facing vs site-only callers and converting the 252/9 split from drift into evidence.

work_item_backlog.title: Lint `lint:no-db-in-handlers` — fail CI on `pgxpool`/`database/sql` import in handlers
work_item_backlog.description: As a devex engineer, I wish to add `dev/scripts/lint_no_db_in_handlers.py` and seed `dev/registries/handler_db_exemptions.txt` with the eight known stragglers, so that CI ratchets every new handler onto the service track and the ledger empties as each extraction lands.

work_item_backlog.title: Extract `auth/handler.go` to `auth.Service`
work_item_backlog.description: As a backend engineer, I wish to extract `Login`, `Refresh`, `Logout` from `backend/internal/auth/handler.go` into `auth.Service`, so that the handler holds only HTTP concerns and the auth row can be removed from the `lint:no-db-in-handlers` exemption ledger. Mount the handler under `/_site/auth`.
work_item_backlog.risk_justification: Auth-critical path; mishandled extraction can break login. Mitigation: feature-flag the new path; dual-mode for one cycle; dedicated Playwright auth smoke before cutover.

work_item_backlog.title: Extract `fields/handler.go` to `fields.Service`
work_item_backlog.description: As a backend engineer, I wish to extract custom-field CRUD from `backend/internal/fields/handler.go` into `fields.Service`, so that the row leaves the `lint:no-db-in-handlers` exemption ledger. Custom fields are staff-managed; pin write surface to `/_site/fields` with read-only on `/samantha/v2/fields`.

work_item_backlog.title: Extract `errorsreport/handler.go` to `errorsreport.Service`
work_item_backlog.description: As a backend engineer, I wish to extract the error-report intake from `backend/internal/errorsreport/handler.go` into `errorsreport.Service`, so that error reporting is a site-only handler under `/_site/errors` and rows leave the exemption ledger. Service writes wire to `audit.Service.Record` once 00531 lands.

work_item_backlog.title: Extract `libraryreleases/handler.go` to `libraryreleases.Service`
work_item_backlog.description: As a backend engineer, I wish to extract library-release reads from `backend/internal/libraryreleases/handler.go` into `libraryreleases.Service`, so that the cross-DB pool pattern lives inside the service and the row leaves the exemption ledger. Mount under `/_site/library/releases`.
work_item_backlog.risk_justification: Cross-DB pool consumer (vector + library DBs). Mitigation: replicate the cross-DB pattern from `entityrefs.Service`; add integration test exercising both pools before cutover.

work_item_backlog.title: Extract `roles/handler.go` reads to `roles.Service`
work_item_backlog.description: As a backend engineer, I wish to fold role-read methods (`List`, `Get`, etc.) from `backend/internal/roles/handler.go` into the existing `roles.Service`, so that reads and writes share one boundary and the row leaves the exemption ledger. Service already exists for writes per `lint:writer-boundary`.

work_item_backlog.title: Extract `portfoliomodels/handler*.go` (×3) and `portfolio/master_record_handler.go` to services
work_item_backlog.description: As a backend engineer, I wish to extract handler bodies from `portfoliomodels/handler.go`, `handler_layers.go`, `handler_workspace_layers.go`, and `portfolio/master_record_handler.go` into their service packages, so that the four-handler bundle leaves the exemption ledger. Coordinate with PLA-0026 to avoid file-collision; bundle into one PR window.
work_item_backlog.risk_justification: Largest straggler set; collides with PLA-0026 (per-workspace adoption cutover) on the same files. Mitigation: bundle into PLA-0026's PR window or block PLA-0026 explicitly on this story.

work_item_backlog.title: `audit_events` table + `audit.Service.Record()` sole-writer with `source_transport`
work_item_backlog.description: As a SOC 2-bound engineer, I wish to add an `audit_events` table (id, tenant_id, actor_user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, request_id, source_transport, before_jsonb, after_jsonb, created_at) and an `audit.Service.Record()` sole-writer, so that every mutating service method records evidence synchronously in-transaction and `source_transport ∈ {site, public}` distinguishes staff actions from customer actions on one auditable surface. `source_transport` is derived from chi `RouteContext` URL prefix by middleware — never from a handler argument. Extend `lint:writer-boundary` so only `audit.Service` writes the table. Wire into the three highest-traffic mutating services (`artefactitemsv2`, `roles`, `orgdesign`).
work_item_backlog.risk_justification: Synchronous in-transaction write on hot mutation paths. Mitigation: start with three services; measure p95; batch only if measurement justifies (no premature optimisation). Failure mode: audit write rolls back parent transaction — a desirable invariant.

work_item_backlog.title: DTO + `MapPublic` mapper convention guarded by `lint:public-dto-mapper`
work_item_backlog.description: As a backend engineer, I wish to formalise a per-service `dto.go` with `MapPublic(internal X) dto.XPublic`, and ship `dev/scripts/lint_public_dto_mapper.py` to fail CI when any handler under `/samantha/v2/*` returns a Go struct from `internal/<svc>` (not `internal/<svc>/dto`), so that internal column additions can never accidentally leak through the customer adapter. `portfoliomodels/dto.go` is the seed example.

work_item_backlog.title: `docs/c_c_transport_segregation.md` — single architectural reference for the split
work_item_backlog.description: As a documentarian, I wish to write `docs/c_c_transport_segregation.md` covering the handler→Service→audit diagram, the URL-prefix rule (`/_site` vs `/samantha/v2`), the three lints (`lint:public-helper-allowlist`, `lint:no-db-in-handlers`, `lint:public-dto-mapper`), the DTO mapper convention, and the SOC 2 evidence story, so that any future engineer (or auditor) can reach the architectural rationale from a single page. Add a one-line pointer in CLAUDE.md alongside `c_c_v1_v2_cutover.md`.

work_item_backlog.title: Document gateway-layer `/_site` ingress rule (deferred to gateway landing)
work_item_backlog.description: As a security engineer, I wish to document in `docs/c_c_transport_segregation.md` that once the gateway from B17.9 lands, `/_site/*` requests originating outside the staff VPN/SSO must 404 at the gateway, so that the security intent is captured before the gateway code exists and ships when the gateway ships.

work_item_backlog.title: Extend `<stories>` skill with `transport: site | public | both` decision gate
work_item_backlog.description: As a process engineer, I wish to extend the `<stories>` skill checklist (`.claude/skills/stories/SKILL.md`) so every new endpoint card declares `transport: site | public | both`, so that the customer-facing-vs-site decision happens at story time — not at handler time — and drift cannot re-emerge.

risks: Risk: handler extractions in Tasks 6–11 collide with PLA-0026 (per-workspace adoption cutover) on portfoliomodels/* — mitigation: bundle Task 11 into PLA-0026's PR window or block PLA-0026 on Task 11 explicitly

Risk: lint:public-helper-allowlist initial seed too permissive — mitigation: seed with strictly current customer-facing files; new entries require reviewer note. Periodically audit ledger size as a regression signal

Risk: audit_events synchronous write becomes a hot-path bottleneck on high-frequency mutating services — mitigation: start with three services, measure p95, batch only if measurement justifies it (no premature batching)
Risk: DTO + MapPublic convention adds boilerplate that gets skipped — mitigation: lint:public-dto-mapper makes it a CI failure, not a guideline; reviewer can't merge around it
Risk: deprecation window on root-level shims gets extended ad-infinitum — mitigation: write the removal commit on the same branch as the shim; gate on a date, not a feature, in CLAUDE.md ledger
Risk: auth handler extraction (Task 6) breaks login if mishandled — mitigation: extract first under feature flag, run dual-mode for one cycle, then cut over; auth has dedicated Playwright coverage

Risk: source_transport column is set at handler time and could be falsified by a handler that mounts on the wrong prefix — mitigation: source_transport derived from chi RouteContext (URL prefix) by middleware, never from handler argument; lint:writer-boundary enforces audit.Record signature
